summary: Sprint 3 backend testing for Sit Happens CRM. 18 new tests validate /api/settings (admin + public + PUT partial), /api/auth/change-password, /api/bookings/recurring, /api/bookings/{id}/reschedule, training service type, settings-driven daycare capacity, auto-approve, cancellation cutoff, multi-vaccine enforcement, and dashboard.training_today. Full regression: 48/48 passing (test_sithappens.py 20 + test_sprint2.py 10 + test_sprint3.py 18).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~970 lines (well past the 700-line guideline) — strongly recommend splitting into routers (auth, clients, dogs, bookings, settings, dashboard) before sprint 4.

Default daycare_capacity is read from env (DAYCARE_CAPACITY) into _default_settings() at module import time, then settings are persisted to MongoDB on first GET. If admin changes daycare_capacity later via env, the seeded doc still wins — minor surprise. Document or remove env fallback.
RescheduleIn allows date == past dates; admin reschedule accepts any string without ISO validation (datetime.fromisoformat is only used in cancel/recurring). Consider validating body.date format.

create_recurring calls create_booking() directly inside a loop and translates HTTPException to {date, reason}. This is fine but emits N independent inserts; consider batching with insert_many for atomicity if a date later in the loop fails.

Cancellation cutoff parses booking['date'] as datetime.fromisoformat(...).replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) — treats YYYY-MM-DD as 00:00 UTC, so a booking 'today' is already past cutoff. Acceptable but verify product intent (cutoff vs. start-of-day vs. business hours).

auto_approve path always succeeds for clients even when daycare_cost > client.credits would normally block them — current code correctly checks insufficient credits BEFORE status decision, so it's fine; just flagging that the order matters and should remain so.

save_settings() does a $set with the whole nested dict (e.g., booking_rules replaces entire sub-doc). Partial updates inside booking_rules require sending the full sub-object — tests already handle this; document in API contract.
DismissIn model still defined and unused (carry-over from iteration_2 review).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/conftest.py (new, session-scoped settings reset for legacy compat)
test_credentials: admin@sithappens.com / admin123. Portal users created per-test with unique emails (s3portal_*, pwtest_*).
seed_data_creation: TEST_S3_* clients/dogs and TEST_S3_KENNEL/TEST_Happy/TEST_Sleepy mood tags created and cleaned up. conftest.py snapshots settings at session start and restores at session end so the global settings doc is left in its original state for the frontend.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Three pytest files in /app/backend/tests/: test_sithappens.py (20 tests, iter1), test_sprint2.py (10 tests, iter2), test_sprint3.py (18 tests, iter3). conftest.py auto-loosens settings to required_vaccines=['rabies'] and restores after the session so legacy tests (which only seed rabies) still pass under Sprint 3 defaults that require rabies+bordetella+dhpp. If running a single legacy test file individually, conftest still applies. One transient connect-timeout was observed against the public preview URL during the long run — rerunning passed cleanly; tests are otherwise deterministic.
